meta.globalOtaStatus: White House-backed pilot program officially announced
meta.bannerMessage: Legal disputes between Joby Aviation and Archer Aviation may impact eIPP projects involving these operators

summaryBlocks.heading: Cargo Signal
summaryBlocks.body: Louisiana, Albuquerque, North Carolina, Utah, and Florida Phase 1 are all cargo or medical logistics. Revenue freight expected Q4 2026 — ahead of any passenger operations. Beta Technologies CEO Kyle Clark confirmed initial eIPP flights will carry cargo and medical supplies before progressing to passengers.

summaryBlocks.heading: Legal Threshold Crossed
summaryBlocks.body: First time in U.S. aviation history that pre-certified aircraft can carry cargo for revenue in commercial airspace under FAA-supervised Other Transaction Agreements. This does not accelerate type certification for passenger ops — it creates a parallel commercial track for cargo.

summaryBlocks.heading: Certification Reality
summaryBlocks.body: eIPP does not change the certification clock. Short sellers Grizzly Research and Culper Research question Archer certification before 2028. Cargo and medical logistics face simpler liability and do not require passenger type certification. Watch the cargo projects — not the air taxis.

projects.sponsor: Port Authority of New York & New Jersey
projects.keyOp: eVTOL passenger operations at Manhattan Heliport; 12 distinct concepts across New England
projects.milestone: Archer Aviation partnership with Hopscotch Air for air taxi testing
projects.notes: Most complex project in the program — 12 distinct operational concepts. Beta Technologies has specifically identified upstate New York as a launch location for cargo and medical logistics missions, building on NUAIR's FAA-accepted 1,900 sq mi airspace network with 6,000+ flights already logged. Joby acquired Blade Air Mobility's passenger division in 2025, giving it existing terminal relationships across the New York area. Electra is studying a NJ-to-NY route with Signature Aviation and Vertiports by Atlantic. Analyst concerns noted regarding additional capital needs despite White House support for eIPP program. Analyst concerns raised regarding additional capital needs despite White House support for eIPP program. Company continues to advance toward OTA despite funding pressures. Analyst concerns raised regarding additional capital needs despite White House support. Project viability being assessed in context of Archer Aviation's financial position. Archer Aviation recognized by White House for real-world eVTOL deployment focus and operations leadership. White House announcement of FAA eIPP air-taxi pilot program boost confirmed. Stock market activity noted despite White House pilot program announcement and recognition. White House recognition for eVTOL operations role, emphasizing real-world implementation focus. Archer Aviation facing ongoing capital pressures despite federal air taxi program progress; company valuation in focus. Leadership changes and funding discipline initiatives underway to support federal air taxi program goals. Archer Aviation progressing on test flights with continued analyst interest and valuation development supporting eIPP participation. Financial viability concerns reported regarding Archer Aviation's stock and operational sustainability. Hopscotch Air partnership with Archer Aviation announced for air mobility testing operations. Archer Aviation's financial and operational recovery progressing; company rebound supports continued project collaboration. New partnership announced between Archer Aviation and Hopscotch Air for expanded air taxi testing operations. Article reports Archer Aviation facing operational challenges and market headwinds in eVTOL sector as air taxi hype fades.

projects.keyOp: Regional air taxi network connecting Dallas, Austin, San Antonio, and Houston
projects.milestone: Demonstration mail delivery flights with Loganair underway
projects.notes: Largest OEM concentration of any single-state project. Wisk pursuing 'autonomy-first' crawl-walk-run strategy: focused ops in dedicated areas → dedicated routes → full Gen 6 high-frequency operations. Wisk CEO Vigneron: 'We are validating the entire digital and physical ecosystem required to support autonomous flight.' Wisk Gen 6 completed first flight December 2025. Archer targeting H2 2026 ops. Short sellers Grizzly Research and Culper Research question Archer certification before 2028. Loganair and BETA Technologies conducting series of demonstration electric mail delivery flights, showing operational progress in electric aircraft deployment. BETA Technologies conducted inaugural electric flight with Loganair in Scotland, validating operational procedures and aircraft performance in real-world conditions. Loganair and BETA Technologies conducting series of demonstration mail delivery electric flights, advancing operational integration of electric aircraft. Loganair conducted first electric flight in Scotland with BETA Technologies, demonstrating operational progress in eVTOL certification and deployment. BETA Technologies conducting series of demonstration mail delivery electric flights with Loganair, advancing operational progress toward eIPP objectives.

projects.region: Pacific NW · Rockies · Plains of Oklahoma
projects.keyOp: Medical deliveries to rural communities across mountainous terrain and wildfire-prone regions
projects.notes: Geographically the most ambitious project — five states, three distinct terrain types. Ampaire will fly a Cesta 208 B Grand Caravan with hybrid-electric upgrade on routes that parallel existing daily airline operations, comparing performance against conventional engine. Strongest cargo-first signal among western projects. Future Flight Global (FFG) is a key implementation partner. Four full seasons of operations including snow, ice, and cold will generate adverse-weather data no other project can match.

projects.keyOp: Revitalize Essential Air Service-equivalent regional routes across 13 states
projects.milestone: Joby eVTOL demonstration showcased over San Francisco
projects.notes: Built on the NASAO AAM Multistate Collaborative — the broadest multi-state cooperative in the program. Electra's EL9 is the key aircraft: nine seats, 150 ft ground roll, operates from grass strips. This opens routes at smaller airports that no conventional regional carrier serves. The EAS replacement angle is the strongest economic argument in the program — a real, documented market failure with measurable demand. Electra CEO Marc Allen: 'flying directly from a parking lot next door to an Atlantic City casino into a major airport.' Joby Aviation conducting air taxi flight operations in the Bay Area, demonstrating operational progress. Recent news reports confirm air taxi flights occurring in Bay Area region, validating operational readiness. NBC Bay Area reported Joby Aviation air taxis conducting flight operations in the Bay Area, confirming active demonstration phase. Joby Aviation conducted piloted air taxi demonstration flights in Bay Area, showcasing operational readiness for eIPP integration. Joby Aviation conducting FAA-conforming tests and participating in eVTOL Pilot Program as noted in industry reporting. Joby Aviation confirmed as participant in FAA eVTOL Pilot Program. FAA-conforming tests successfully conducted. Joby Aviation conducted public demonstration of urban air mobility capabilities over San Francisco, showcasing eVTOL technology for future implementation.

projects.region: Gulf of America · Offshore Energy Sector
projects.keyOp: Cargo and personnel flights to offshore energy platforms in the Gulf of America
projects.editorial: The Louisiana offshore energy project deserves close attention. Bristow Group, the operator partner, has been coordinating with the FAA on the Elroy Air Chaparral for over a year. When the helicopter industry's dominant offshore operator validates autonomous cargo as a viable replacement for manned supply runs, the cost calculus changes for every operator in the Gulf.
projects.notes: Elroy Air Chaparral: fully autonomous, 300 lb payload, 300-mile range, no pilot, no passenger. Bristow Group is the customer and long-time partner, providing operational credibility. Offshore energy helicopter costs run $3,000–$8,000/flight for platform supply runs — the economic pain point is real and institutional payers are already in place. Elroy Air CEO Andrew Clare: 'That doesn't happen without a truly mission-ready aircraft and a team that's been doing this work for years.'

projects.keyOp: Three-phase: Phase 1 cargo, Phase 2 passenger, Phase 3 automation and medical response
projects.notes: Largest stated public and private investment commitment in the program. The three-phase structure is explicit validation of the cargo-first sequencing thesis: cargo operations are Phase 1 because they carry lower regulatory burden and simpler liability. Florida's dense urban network (Miami, Orlando, Tampa, Jacksonville) makes it the strongest eventual passenger market. Having the state explicitly sequence cargo before passengers is a blueprint for how other markets should structure AAM development.

projects.keyOp: Piloted medical and regional ops statewide; autonomous flight corridor extending into Virginia
projects.notes: Medical transport is the primary use case — institutional payers (hospital systems, organ procurement organizations), defined corridors, measurable time savings over ground transport. Joby Organ Transport program directly aligns with this project. The autonomous extension into Virginia is a deliberate regulatory data play — NC and VA together create a cross-state autonomous corridor generating FAA policy data on autonomous ops in non-segregated airspace. Beta's medical logistics experience from existing operations is the credentialing asset.

projects.region: New Mexico · Class D + C airports
projects.keyOp: Autonomous cargo ops at Class D and C airports — revenue freight, no pilot, no passengers
projects.notes: The most strategically significant project for the cargo-first thesis. Reliable Robotics has already been operating in the region and coordinating with the FAA — it is not starting from scratch. Autonomous commercial freight in controlled airspace is the use case drone delivery operators have been chasing under Part 107 for years. If Reliable logs the first autonomous revenue cargo flight under eIPP, it sets a precedent that will reframe the regulatory conversation around uncrewed logistics. Wisk CEO Dan Dalton confirmed Wisk itself may fly helicopters first to collect operational data — signaling that even autonomy-focused companies see the crawl-walk-run approach as necessary. North Carolina Department of Transportation selected for FAA eIPP program.
